title: Fix timeframe propagation bug: Settings selection not reflected in Results screen

requirements.text: Fix the localStorage key mismatch between frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx and frontend/src/services/localCandleAnalysis.ts. Audit both files to confirm they use the exact same key string when writing and reading the timeframe value. If the keys differ, unify them to a single constant (e.g., 'settings_timeframe'). The Settings page must write the selected value ('M1', 'M3', or 'M5') to localStorage under this key immediately on selection, and localCandleAnalysis.ts must read from this exact same key at analysis execution time — never at module load time.

quotes: o que você quis dizer foi: Ao selecionar 5 minutos nas configurações, o timeframe deveria mudar em todos os lugares do app

acceptanceCriteria: The localStorage key used to save the timeframe in Settings.tsx is identical to the key used to read it in localCandleAnalysis.ts
Selecting M5 in Settings persists 'M5' to localStorage under the unified key
Selecting M3 in Settings persists 'M3' to localStorage under the unified key
localCandleAnalysis.ts reads the timeframe at the moment analyzeChartImage() is called, not at import/module load time
The LocalAnalysisResult.timeframe field contains 'M5' when user had M5 selected, 'M3' when M3 was selected, 'M1' when M1 was selected

requirements.text: Fix the Settings page timeframe selector in frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx so that selecting M3 or M5 immediately updates the displayed/active selected state in the UI, persists the value to localStorage under the unified key (matching localCandleAnalysis.ts), and saves to the backend via the updateSettings mutation. The selector must not visually revert to its previous value after selection. On page reload, the selector must restore the last saved value. Show the Portuguese success toast 'Configuração salva!' after each successful save.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting M5 in the timeframe selector immediately shows M5 as the active/selected option without reverting
Selecting M3 in the timeframe selector immediately shows M3 as the active/selected option without reverting
The selected value is written to localStorage immediately on change
The updateSettings mutation is called with the new timeframe value
'Configuração salva!' toast appears after successful save
Navigating away and returning to Settings shows the previously saved timeframe value

requirements.text: Fix the countdown timer and Time Frame tile in frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx to use the timeframe value from the analysis result's 'timeframe' field. The timer initial duration must be: M1 = 60 seconds, M3 = 180 seconds, M5 = 300 seconds. The 'Entrar às HH:MM' entry time must be computed as the current time at page load plus the correct duration. The Time Frame tile in the 2×2 info grid must display the value from the analysis result's timeframe field (e.g., 'M5'), never hardcoding or defaulting to M1 when another timeframe was selected. Also update frontend/src/hooks/useCountdownTimer.ts to accept the timeframe-derived duration correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: When analysis result contains timeframe='M5', the countdown timer starts at 05:00 (300 seconds)
When analysis result contains timeframe='M3', the countdown timer starts at 03:00 (180 seconds)
When analysis result contains timeframe='M1', the countdown timer starts at 01:00 (60 seconds)
The Time Frame tile displays 'M5' when the analysis result timeframe is M5, not 'M1'
The Time Frame tile displays 'M3' when the analysis result timeframe is M3
'Entrar às HH:MM' shows current time + correct timeframe duration (e.g., +5 min for M5)
The Time Frame tile subtitle shows '5 min/vela' for M5, '3 min/vela' for M3, '1 min/vela' for M1

constraints: Do not change any backend logic — this is a frontend-only fix
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
All user-facing text must remain in Portuguese (Brazil)
Do not remove or alter any other working features (timer UI, signal banner, grid layout, chart thumbnail, WIN/LOSS buttons)
nonGoals: Adding new timeframe options beyond M1, M3, M5
